glossary.es: Proceso en el que la informacion del ADN se copia en ARN mensajero.
glossary.en: The process in which information in DNA is copied into messenger RNA (mRNA).

glossary.es: Proceso en el que el ribosoma usa el ARN mensajero para formar una proteina.
glossary.en: The process in which a ribosome reads mRNA to build a protein.

glossary.es: Molecula de ARN que lleva instrucciones del ADN al ribosoma.
glossary.en: RNA molecule that carries genetic instructions from DNA to the ribosome.

glossary.es: Estructura celular donde se ensamblan proteinas.
glossary.en: Cell structure where proteins are assembled from amino acids.

glossary.es: Fluido dentro de la celula donde ocurren muchas reacciones.
glossary.en: Gel-like fluid inside the cell where many reactions occur.

glossary.es: Conjunto de tres bases del ARN que codifica un aminoacido.
glossary.en: A three-base sequence in mRNA that codes for an amino acid.

glossary.es: Triplete en el ARNt que se empareja con un codon del ARNm.
glossary.en: A three-base sequence in tRNA that pairs with an mRNA codon.

cards.label: RNA polymerase uses DNA as a template to build mRNA
mRNA moves to a ribosome in the cytoplasm
tRNA matches anticodons to mRNA codons
A chain of amino acids forms a protein

items.title: DNA → RNA → Protein Flow

stimulus.text: Complete the model of how genetic information becomes a trait.

items.prompt: Select the correct answer for each box in the flow diagram.

items.title: Prokaryotic vs Eukaryotic Structure

stimulus.text: A student observes a cell with an electron microscope.

items.prompt: Which feature most strongly indicates the cell is [[prokaryotic|key=procariota]]?

glossary.es: Celula sin nucleo definido ni organelos membranosos.
glossary.en: A cell type that lacks a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

glossary.es: Celula con nucleo definido y organelos membranosos.
glossary.en: A cell type that has a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

glossary.es: Organelo que contiene el ADN en celulas eucariotas.
glossary.en: Organelle in eukaryotic cells that stores most of the cell's DNA.

glossary.es: Organelo donde ocurre la fotosintesis en plantas.
glossary.en: Plant cell organelle where photosynthesis takes place.

glossary.es: Organelo que produce ATP por respiracion celular.
glossary.en: Organelle where most ATP is produced during cellular respiration.

choices.label: A nucleus surrounded by a membrane
DNA located in the cytoplasm without a nucleus

items.title: Photosynthesis Reactants
items.prompt: Which substances are required as [[reactants|key=reactivos]] for [[photosynthesis|key=fotosintesis]] to occur?

glossary.es: Sustancias iniciales que se consumen en una reaccion quimica.
glossary.en: Starting substances that are used up in a chemical reaction.

glossary.es: Proceso por el cual las plantas convierten luz en energia quimica.
glossary.en: Process by which plants use light, water, and carbon dioxide to make glucose.

glossary.es: Gas (CO2) usado por las plantas para producir glucosa.
glossary.en: Gas (CO2) that plants take in as a reactant for photosynthesis.

glossary.es: Molecula H2O necesaria en muchas reacciones biologicas.
glossary.en: H2O molecule needed as a reactant in photosynthesis and many cell processes.

glossary.es: Azucar simple producida durante la fotosintesis.
glossary.en: Simple sugar produced by photosynthesis and used by cells for energy.

items.title: Photosynthesis vs. Cellular Respiration
items.prompt: Sort each statement into the correct process: [[photosynthesis|key=fotosintesis]] or [[cellular respiration|key=respiracion_celular]].

glossary.es: Proceso que usa luz para formar glucosa a partir de CO2 y agua.
glossary.en: Photosynthesis

glossary.es: Proceso que descompone glucosa para obtener ATP.
glossary.en: Process that breaks down glucose to release usable energy as ATP.

glossary.es: Organelo donde ocurre la fotosintesis.
glossary.en: Chloroplast

glossary.es: Organelo donde ocurre principalmente la respiracion celular.
glossary.en: Mitochondrion

glossary.es: Molecula que almacena y transfiere energia util en la celula.
glossary.en: Molecule that stores and transfers usable energy in cells.

cards.label: Requires CO₂ and H₂O as reactants
Produces ATP for direct cell use

items.title: Biomolecule Monomers

stimulus.text: Large biological molecules (macromolecules) are assembled from smaller repeating subunits called monomers.

items.prompt: Which of the following are [[monomers|key=monomeros]] that make up larger [[macromolecules|key=macromoleculas]]? Select ALL that apply.

glossary.es: Subunidades pequenas que se unen para formar polimeros.
glossary.en: Small building-block molecules that join together to form polymers.

glossary.es: Moleculas biologicas grandes como proteinas, carbohidratos y acidos nucleicos.
glossary.en: Large biological molecules such as proteins, carbohydrates, lipids, and nucleic acids.

glossary.es: Monomero que forma proteinas.
glossary.en: Monomer that links together to form proteins.

glossary.es: Monomero que forma ADN y ARN.
glossary.en: Monomer that makes up DNA and RNA.

glossary.es: Azucar simple, monomero de varios carbohidratos.
glossary.en: Glucose

items.title: Control Group Identification

stimulus.text: A student tests whether fertilizer increases plant height. She grows 10 plants with fertilizer and 10 plants with only water, keeping all other conditions identical.

items.prompt: Which group of plants is the [[control group|key=grupo_control]]?

glossary.es: Grupo que no recibe el tratamiento experimental y sirve de comparacion.
glossary.en: Group in an experiment that does not receive the treatment and is used for comparison.

glossary.es: Factor que puede cambiar en un experimento.
glossary.en: A factor in an experiment that can change or be manipulated.

glossary.es: Explicacion o prediccion que puede ponerse a prueba.
glossary.en: A testable explanation or prediction based on observations.

glossary.es: Informacion recolectada durante un experimento.
glossary.en: Recorded observations and measurements collected during an investigation.

choices.label: The 10 plants grown with only water
Both groups together form the control
The student herself is the control

items.title: Energy Loss in Food Chains
items.prompt: Why does available [[energy|key=energia]] decrease at each higher [[trophic level|key=nivel_trofico]] in a food chain?

glossary.es: Capacidad de realizar trabajo en sistemas biologicos.
glossary.en: The ability to do work, transferred through biological systems.

glossary.es: Posicion de un organismo en una cadena alimentaria.
glossary.en: A feeding position in a food chain or food web.

glossary.es: Conjunto de reacciones quimicas que ocurren en un organismo.
glossary.en: All chemical reactions in an organism that sustain life.

glossary.es: Ruta por la cual la energia pasa de un organismo a otro.
glossary.en: A sequence showing how energy passes from one organism to another.

choices.label: Organisms at higher trophic levels eat less food
Energy is lost as heat during metabolic processes
Higher-level organisms are always smaller in size
Sunlight cannot reach higher trophic levels

items.title: Natural Selection in a Population

stimulus.text: A population of beetles lives on dark tree bark. Green beetles are easily spotted and eaten by birds, while dark-brown beetles blend in and survive to reproduce.

items.prompt: Which outcome best describes what will happen to this beetle [[population|key=poblacion]] over many generations due to [[natural selection|key=seleccion_natural]]?

glossary.es: Grupo de organismos de la misma especie que vive en una zona.
glossary.en: Members of the same species living in the same area at the same time.

glossary.es: Proceso por el cual rasgos ventajosos se vuelven mas comunes.
glossary.en: Process where individuals with advantageous traits are more likely to survive and reproduce.

glossary.es: Rasgo heredable que mejora la supervivencia o reproduccion.
glossary.en: An inherited trait that improves survival or reproduction in a specific environment.

glossary.es: Cambio en la secuencia del ADN.
glossary.en: A change in DNA sequence that can introduce new traits.

choices.label: All beetles will eventually learn to avoid predators
The proportion of dark-brown beetles will increase over time
Green beetles will mutate to become dark-brown within one generation
The total population size will remain unchanged

items.title: Tt × Tt Cross: Dominant Phenotype %

stimulus.text: Tall (T) is dominant over short (t) in pea plants. Two heterozygous tall plants (Tt) are crossed.

items.prompt: What percentage of offspring from a [[heterozygous|key=heterocigoto]] cross are expected to show the TALL [[phenotype|key=fenotipo]]? Enter a whole number.

glossary.es: Individuo con dos alelos diferentes para un gen (por ejemplo, Tt).
glossary.en: Having two different alleles for a gene (for example, Tt).

glossary.es: Caracteristica observable de un organismo.
glossary.en: Observable characteristics of an organism.

glossary.es: Combinacion de alelos que posee un organismo.
glossary.en: Genetic makeup of an organism; the allele combination it carries.

glossary.es: Version alternativa de un gen.
glossary.en: One version of a gene at a specific locus.

items.title: Photosynthesis Evidence Pair

stimulus.text: A scientist places a plant in a sealed container with elevated CO₂. After 24 hours in sunlight, the CO₂ level drops and the plant's mass increases.

items.prompt:
Part A: Which process ([[photosynthesis|key=fotosintesis]] or [[cellular respiration|key=respiracion_celular]]) best explains the decrease in CO₂?
Part B: Which molecule is the primary organic product (for example [[glucose|key=glucosa]])?

glossary.es: Proceso por el cual las plantas usan luz para sintetizar glucosa.
glossary.en: Photosynthesis

glossary.es: Proceso que convierte la energia de la glucosa en ATP.
glossary.en: Cellular respiration

glossary.es: Azucar producida por fotosintesis y usada como fuente de energia.
glossary.en: Glucose

glossary.es: Dioxido de carbono, gas reactivo en fotosintesis.
glossary.en: Carbon dioxide
